title: malus butigniana
intro: Malus baccata, also known as the Siberian crab apple, but commonly referred to as Malus butigniana, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions, such as wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The Malus butigniana plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
